payload.text:
Tu es une IA experte en branding, identité visuelle et design graphique professionnel,
avec un niveau équivalent à une agence de branding haut de gamme.

🎯 OBJECTIF PRINCIPAL
Créer un logo STRICTEMENT PROFESSIONNEL et une charte graphique complète,
à partir UNIQUEMENT des informations fournies sur l’entreprise ou le business.

🧠 RÈGLES FONDAMENTALES
- Aucun jargon compliqué : langage simple, clair et accessible
- Explications compréhensibles même pour un non-designer
- Approche structurée, pédagogique et user-friendly
- Résultat prêt à être utilisé par un graphiste ou dans des logiciels comme Affinity, Adobe ou Figma
- Le rendu doit inspirer confiance, crédibilité et professionnalisme

📥 INPUT (les seules informations autorisées)
- Nom de l’entreprise
- Secteur d’activité
- Mission / vision
- Valeurs clés
- Cible principale (clients)
- Positionnement (luxe, moderne, technologique, accessible, institutionnel, etc.)
- Préférences éventuelles (couleurs, symboles à éviter ou à privilégier)

🎨 ÉTAPE 1 : CONCEPTION DU LOGO
Proposer :
1. Le concept du logo (signification, message, intention)
2. Le type de logo recommandé :
   - Logotype
   - Logomark
   - Monogramme
   - Combinaison
3. La symbolique des formes et du style
4. Une description précise permettant une reproduction fidèle du logo
5. Les déclinaisons recommandées :
   - Couleur
   - Noir
   - Blanc
   - Fond clair / fond foncé

📘 ÉTAPE 2 : CHARTE GRAPHIQUE (BONUS OBLIGATOIRE)
Créer une charte graphique professionnelle incluant :
- Palette de couleurs (codes HEX + usage)
- Typographies recommandées (titres / textes)
- Règles d’utilisation du logo (espaces, tailles, interdictions)
- Style visuel global (moderne, minimaliste, corporate, tech, etc.)
- Applications possibles :
  - Réseaux sociaux
  - Flyers
  - Cartes de visite
  - Site web

🧩 FORMAT DE SORTIE
- Structuré
- Clair
- Professionnel
- Facile à comprendre
- Prêt à être transmis à un graphiste ou à une équipe marketing

⚠️ INTERDICTION
- Ne jamais inventer d’informations
- Ne jamais demander d’éléments supplémentaires sauf si absolument nécessaire
- Ne jamais produire un design amateur ou générique

🎯 OBJECTIF FINAL
Fournir une identité visuelle professionnelle complète,
crédible et cohérente, adaptée au business décrit.

**Developing the Core Concept**

I'm zeroing in on the core functionality: transforming business input into tangible branding outputs. Currently, I'm focusing on defining the scope of "world-class" and determining the data requirements for logo generation and style guide creation. I'm also considering the user experience for seamless interaction.


**Refining the Input Module**

I'm now deeply engrossed in the input form design. I've defined the key fields: company name, sector, mission, values, target audience, positioning, and preferences (colors, symbols). I envision using the Gemini API to process these inputs. The user interface will be modern, possibly with a multi-step form to avoid overwhelming. React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS seem like the perfect tools, alongside `gemini-3-flash-preview` for text.


**Visualizing the Output**

I'm now thinking about the UI components. I'm planning out `index.html`, `index.tsx`, and `App.tsx` files. I have `types.ts` set up with the interfaces for business profiles and results. I also have component files: `InputForm.tsx`, `BrandingResult.tsx`, `ColorCard.tsx`, and `Header.tsx`. I'll be using `services/geminiService.ts` for API handling. A professional agency tone is the system instruction, and a JSON format is crucial. The translation for the input form is next. French output is also going to happen. I am considering a sophisticated color palette.


**Crafting the Implementation Details**

Now I'm tackling the technical build. I'm focusing on the service layer and UI component development: creating the form and the detailed report view. Tailwind CSS will ensure a premium look. Proper use of `GoogleGenAI` with the API key is a priority, as is confirming `gemini-3-flash-preview` is configured. Also, I'm verifying the JSON response configuration. Accessibility and responsiveness are also important.

payload.text: THE TEXT INPUT SHOULD BE IN BLACK AND NOT WHITE

**Adjusting Input Fields**

I'm currently focused on the `InputForm.tsx` component. The white text (or unreadable text) in the input fields needs to be black. I'm reviewing the existing styling, particularly the `inputClasses` constant, to make the changes there. My goal is to improve the readability of these form elements.


**Clarifying Text Color**

I've zeroed in on the `inputClasses` within `InputForm.tsx`. My previous assumption that it implicitly set a text color was incorrect. Adding `text-gray-900` or `text-black` to the `inputClasses` appears to be the most direct solution, addressing the core issue of white text on a white background. I'm verifying that no global styles or forced dark mode are interfering. I will update `components/InputForm.tsx` to include `text-gray-900` in the `inputClasses`. I'll also check for any other form elements needing attention.


**Finalizing Input Styling**

I'm now revising the `inputClasses` within `InputForm.tsx`. My current plan is to use `text-black` to guarantee the text color is dark. While `text-gray-900` would have worked, `text-black` is the strongest assertion of intent. I am now updating all the form elements to verify this change applies consistently to ensure a fully legible experience. I will check for other form fields needing attention.


**Refining Color Choice**

I've decided to revert back to `text-gray-900` for the input field text. This shade aligns better with the desired "high-end branding" aesthetic, offering a slightly softer look than pure black. I'm now verifying that the placeholder text is also appropriately styled. I will change the placeholder text to `placeholder-gray-400`. I am also implementing these updates.

**Initiating Brand Image Generation**

I'm now focused on generating logo images. My plan is to utilize the Gemini Image Generation API, specifically the `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model, to produce visual representations based on the textual logo descriptions. This will be the initial step to make mockups, from which branding decisions will flow.


**Visualizing Brand Identity**

I'm now integrating image generation into the branding workflow. My current focus is to leverage the `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model to produce visual mockups, like business cards and website previews, alongside the logo. I've updated the `geminiService.ts` file with an image generation function. The next step will be to display these images alongside the textual descriptions, now that their URLs are added in state. Lastly, I will improve the print style sheet, given user feedback.


**Fixing the PDF Print**

I've been working on the PDF export functionality, recognizing that the "Export to PDF" action is fundamentally a print operation in the browser. To address potential display issues during printing, I'm adding a specific print-only stylesheet. This should eliminate unwanted elements like headers and buttons, ensuring a clean, professional PDF output.

**Addressing Export Concerns**

I'm currently wrestling with this PDF export issue. The existing `window.print()` method isn't cutting it for a clean PDF. I'm investigating alternative approaches to ensure the branding guide exports flawlessly, considering it needs to look professional when printed. Maybe an external library is the answer.


**Refining PDF Output**

I'm now zeroing in on the PDF export. I've re-examined the initial `window.print()` approach within `BrandingResult.tsx`, given the user's specific feedback about export failure. I'm focusing now on hardening the print styles for PDFs and explicitly guiding users on how to "print to PDF" with clearer instructions. While I have concerns about heavy libraries, I'm considering light-weight solutions. I'm also considering the potential for `window.print()` not working as expected on all devices/browsers.


**Implement PDF Download**

I've shifted gears. The `window.print()` method's limitations have prompted a switch to a more robust solution. I'm now integrating `html2pdf.js` via CDN to produce a genuine downloadable PDF. This involves updating `index.html` and `BrandingResult.tsx`. I need to ensure images render correctly and completely load before export. I will then ensure the images display the logo and mockup, refining prompts if needed.


**Optimizing Download Process**

I'm now fully immersed in the download functionality. I've switched the button action to trigger an `html2pdf` download, keeping `window.print()` as a fallback for text quality. I'm carefully reviewing the `index.html` imports to ensure `html2pdf.js` is correctly included. I'm also refining the download handler in `BrandingResult.tsx`. I'm verifying the images, particularly the logo and mockups, render well with `html2pdf`, as the Gemini-generated base64 images should work perfectly. I may improve the prompts for image quality.


**Improving Image Fidelity**

I'm now focusing on image quality and professional aesthetics within the PDF. I'm leveraging `html2pdf` to refine the layout for the logo and mockups. I'm also revising the image generation prompts in `services/geminiService.ts` to ensure a highly professional output. This ensures the output is very clean. I've also verified that the text is black.

**Considering Logo Variations**

I'm focusing on expanding the branding guide's "Logo" section. I'm thinking of incorporating simplified, monochrome, and favicon variations to enrich it. The existing structure touches on "variations," but I'll need to expand the capabilities to generate corresponding logo images beyond just the standard version.


**Developing Image Generation Strategy**

I'm now diving into the implementation details. I'm updating `types.ts` to include the new image URLs. Next, the `geminiService.ts` function calls will be adjusted, and `App.tsx` will orchestrate the generation of the additional logo variations (simplified, monochrome, favicon). Finally, `BrandingResult.tsx` needs a visual update for displaying them. I'm mindful of potential API limits with the image generation requests and will use prompt refinements to address them.


**Adapting Generation Prompts**

I've realized that the image generation needs adjustments. Gemini can't edit existing logos directly; instead, it creates new images from scratch based on a prompt. This means I have to refine prompts to ensure the generated variations maintain the brand's identity. I'll include the brand name, industry, and core colors to keep them consistent. I am now updating `App.tsx` and adding a "Variations" sub-section in `BrandingResult.tsx`. I also need to make sure the images load before the PDF export.


**Implementing UI Additions**

I'm now implementing the UI adjustments, adding simplified, monochrome, and favicon logo variations, as suggested. I'm also planning to troubleshoot potential `html2pdf` issues by ensuring its proper integration, since it was previously added via CDN. I'm confident that this approach will deliver a comprehensive branding guide.

I'm adding a button in `BrandingResult.tsx`, that triggers these additional mockups. I'm thinking of creating a new `extraMockups` state, or passing it down. Then, I will update `types.ts` to reflect the new structure, and adjust the `App.tsx` and `geminiService.ts` accordingly.


**Prompting Mockup Refinement**

My focus has shifted to refining the prompts for the mockup generation. I've crafted specific instructions for three distinct mockups: a website header, a social media profile picture, and a brochure cover. The goal is to ensure high-quality, professional mockups reflecting modern branding. The user will be able to trigger this functionality from `BrandingResult`. payload.text:
Tu es une IA de branding et d’identité visuelle
fonctionnant comme un Directeur Artistique Senior
avec plus de 20 ans d’expérience en design de marque,
branding stratégique et identité visuelle premium.

Tu travailles au niveau d’une agence internationale haut de gamme.

════════════════════════════════════════
🎯 OBJECTIF PRINCIPAL
════════════════════════════════════════

Créer un LOGO STRICTEMENT PROFESSIONNEL
et une CHARTE GRAPHIQUE COMPLÈTE,
crédible, intemporelle et élégante,
à partir UNIQUEMENT des informations fournies sur l’entreprise.

Le résultat doit être digne d’un designer expert reconnu,
jamais amateur, jamais générique, jamais “IA-like”.

════════════════════════════════════════
🧠 PHILOSOPHIE DE TRAVAIL (OBLIGATOIRE)
════════════════════════════════════════

- Tu réfléchis avant de créer
- Tu expliques tes choix comme un expert, pas comme une IA
- Tu refuses toute idée faible, banale ou à la mode
- Tu privilégies la sobriété, la clarté et la force visuelle
- Tu penses long terme (minimum 10 ans de pertinence)
- Tu conçois des logos exploitables sur tous supports

Tu agis comme un designer senior qui sait dire NON
lorsqu’un choix affaiblit la crédibilité de la marque.

════════════════════════════════════════
🧩 MODE INTERACTIF – UX PREMIUM
════════════════════════════════════════

Tu travailles par ÉTAPES.
Tu ne passes JAMAIS à l’étape suivante sans validation utilisateur.
Tu utilises un langage clair, simple et pédagogique,
accessible même à quelqu’un sans connaissance en design.

Au début, demande :
👉 « Souhaites-tu le MODE SIMPLE ou le MODE EXPERT ? »

- MODE SIMPLE : explications courtes, décisions guidées
- MODE EXPERT : justifications avancées et raisonnement détaillé

════════════════════════════════════════
📥 ÉTAPE 1 – COMPRÉHENSION DU BUSINESS
════════════════════════════════════════

Pose UNIQUEMENT ces questions :

1. Nom exact de l’entreprise ou du projet :
2. Secteur d’activité :
3. Mission principale (1 phrase simple) :
4. Valeurs clés (3 à 5 mots) :
5. Cible principale :
6. Positionnement souhaité
   (moderne, luxe, technologique, institutionnel, accessible, autre)

Ne fais AUCUNE proposition graphique à cette étape.
Attends la réponse de l’utilisateur.

════════════════════════════════════════
🎯 ÉTAPE 2 – ORIENTATION CRÉATIVE
════════════════════════════════════════

- Résume le business en 3 lignes maximum
- Propose 2 ou 3 orientations visuelles PROFESSIONNELLES

Pour chaque orientation, explique :
- Le style
- Le message perçu inconsciemment
- Le type de clients que cela attire

Demande à l’utilisateur de choisir UNE orientation
ou d’en ajuster une.

════════════════════════════════════════
🎨 ÉTAPE 3 – CONCEPTION DU LOGO
════════════════════════════════════════

Sur la base de l’orientation validée :

1. Définis le concept du logo
2. Explique la symbolique (formes, équilibre, intention)
3. Recommande le type de logo
   (logotype, logomark, monogramme, combinaison)
4. Décris le logo avec précision
   (pour reproduction fidèle par un graphiste)
5. Propose les déclinaisons :
   - Couleur
   - Noir
   - Blanc
   - Fond clair / fond foncé

Présente le logo comme lors d’un rendez-vous client professionnel.
Attends validation.

════════════════════════════════════════
🎨 ÉTAPE 4 – PALETTE DE COULEURS
════════════════════════════════════════

- Propose une palette cohérente et professionnelle
- Fournis les codes HEX
- Explique l’usage de chaque couleur
  (principale, secondaire, accent)

Attends validation.

════════════════════════════════════════
✍️ ÉTAPE 5 – TYPOGRAPHIES
════════════════════════════════════════

- Propose :
  - 1 police pour les titres
  - 1 police pour les textes
- Explique pourquoi ces choix renforcent la crédibilité du logo
- Privilégie des polices accessibles et professionnelles

Attends validation.

════════════════════════════════════════
📘 ÉTAPE 6 – CHARTE GRAPHIQUE COMPLÈTE
════════════════════════════════════════

Génère une charte graphique haut de gamme incluant :

- Présentation du logo
- Règles d’utilisation (espaces, tailles, interdictions)
- Palette couleurs officielle
- Typographies
- Style visuel global
- Applications :
  - Réseaux sociaux
  - Flyers
  - Cartes de visite
  - Site web

════════════════════════════════════════
🧪 CONTRÔLES QUALITÉ INTERNES (INVISIBLES)
════════════════════════════════════════

Avant toute présentation finale :
- Teste la lisibilité à petite taille
- Teste le logo sur plusieurs supports
- Vérifie la cohérence visuelle globale
- Élimine toute faiblesse graphique

════════════════════════════════════════
📊 BONUS FINAL
════════════════════════════════════════

- Attribue un score de crédibilité de marque sur 100
- Donne des recommandations d’évolution future

════════════════════════════════════════
🚫 INTERDICTIONS ABSOLUES
════════════════════════════════════════

- Ne jamais inventer d’informations
- Ne jamais produire un logo générique
- Ne jamais être approximatif
- Ne jamais avancer sans validation utilisateur

════════════════════════════════════════
🎯 OBJECTIF FINAL
════════════════════════════════════════

Ce nouveau cahier des charges transforme radicalement l'application pour passer d'un simple générateur à un **véritable atelier de branding interactif** dirigé par un Directeur Artistique Senior. 

### Spécifications de la mise à jour

1.  **Architecture de l'UX (Multi-étapes)** :

A better approach is to provide a "Source Pack" with an SVG, manifest (JSON or Text), and high-res images. I've realized a well-structured PDF or SVG container is the most universally editable option from the browser. However, a ZIP archive with raw images and design specs would be better. *   **Status Tracking** : Une barre de progression granulaire informe l'utilisateur de l'étape précise de l'ingénierie (ex: "Calcul des rapports de proportion...", "Rendu cinématique 1080p...").

**(ii) Architecture des Tracés (Advanced Blueprint) :**
*   **Grille de Construction 2.0** : Le logo n'est plus simplement affiché ; il est disséqué. J'ai ajouté des arcs de tangentes, des lignes de rappel mathématiques, des marqueurs de points d'ancrage et des annotations de stabilité (Ratio $\phi$).
*   **Esthétique CAO** : Un rendu "Blueprint" profond sur fond bleu nuit avec des tracés cyan néon pour prouver la rigueur technique du design.

**(iii) Spécimen Typographique d'Élite :**
*   **Type Specimen Gallery** : Une nouvelle section dédiée affichant le jeu de caractères complet (A-Z, 0-9, glyphes) pour les polices sélectionnées, simulant une fonderie typographique professionnelle.

**(iv) Écosystème Cinématique (Veo 3.1) :**
*   **Motion Master Asset** : Intégration fluide de la vidéo promotionnelle générée par Veo au cœur de la charte graphique, avec un cadre "Digital Billboard" pour une immersion totale.

I'll need to modify `BrandingResult.tsx` to include state for `selectedSections` and implement the selection UI.


**Designing UI Enhancements**

I'm now zeroing in on the UI for the selective export feature. I'm leaning toward minimalist, high-end toggle buttons with a "glass-dark" or clean white aesthetic, ideally placed just above the primary "Exporter le Brand Book" button. I'm also planning to refine the XML further to be more elegant.

payload.text: ajoute des bouttons reseaux sociaux pour permettre a nos differents clients de nous suivrent sur nos different page sociale

**Designing Social Integration**

I'm currently focused on integrating social media buttons into the application. My initial plan is to add links in the `index.html` and use TypeScript in `index.tsx`. I have to decide where the buttons should best fit within the existing UI. I must consider how this would impact the application structure, keeping UI/UX design in mind.


**Organizing the Components**

My thought process has shifted toward organizing the components. I've decided to place the social media links within the `Footer` section of `App.tsx`. I'm going to create social media icons, and I'm currently thinking of using SVGs to keep the application responsive. I plan on making the design modern and sleek using either glassmorphism or a dark theme with hover effects.


**Refining Social Media Integration**

I've revised the footer plan. I'll maintain the current structure and update the `Footer` in `App.tsx`. I now plan to incorporate a `socialLinks` array and utilize high-quality SVGs for the icons. I'm focusing on hover animations for a better user experience. I've also refined the request, considering social media buttons to allow clients to follow us on our different social pages. I am also adding a specification.


**Integrating Footer Icons**

I'm currently updating the `App.tsx` file to include the social icons. I've decided to stick to the footer, as it's the standard location, aiming for a professional look. The icons will be arranged in a spaced horizontal row. I'm planning to implement hover effects, scaling up the icons and changing the color to indigo-400 or white on hover.

C'est une excellente initiative. Pour une agence de branding de ce calibre, la présence sociale doit être affichée avec autant de soin que l'identité de marque elle-même. 

Voici les détails de la mise à jour :
1.  **Emplacement Stratégique** : Les boutons sont intégrés dans le footer pour une accessibilité permanente sans distraire l'utilisateur du flux de travail principal.
2.  **Design "Absolute"** : Utilisation d'icônes vectorielles épurées avec un effet de survol (hover) incluant une lueur indigo (*glow effect*) et une légère rotation dynamique.
3.  **Accessibilité** : Ajout d'attributs `aria-label` pour les lecteurs d'écran et `rel="noopener noreferrer"` pour la sécurité.

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:

```
{"error":{"code":429,"message":"You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, head to: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/rate-limits. To monitor your current usage, head to: https://ai.dev/rate-limit. ","status":"RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED"}}
```

**Investigating API Limits**

I'm currently focused on the `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` error. It seems like the API quota is being exceeded. I am carefully reviewing the `@google/genai` guidelines to understand best practices for error handling and API key management, especially for high-quality models. My aim is to implement a robust solution.


**Refining Error Handling Strategy**

I'm now zeroing in on handling the `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` error alongside the "Requested entity not found" scenario. My approach is to implement both user-facing messages and retry mechanisms for 429 errors within the web application context. In instances of a "Requested entity not found" error, key selection should be reset using the `openSelectKey()` function, as it relates to user-supplied API keys for video generation or image preview models. This ensures a more resilient and user-friendly experience, focusing specifically on video generation and `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` models, in the flow where users provide their own keys.


**Clarifying Error Responses**

I'm working to enhance the error handling. I'll provide clear UI messages to address quota and billing problems. If a quota issue happens, and the user provided the key, I will incorporate a button for re-selecting the API key, following the "user-selected key" flow. I'm making sure to address all API Key Selection rules, incorporating insights from the provided instructions.


**Streamlining Error Management**

I'm now focused on implementing a more robust 429 error handling in `App.tsx`. I'll present a specific message and a button to change the API key using `window.aistudio.openSelectKey()` when `window.aistudio` is available, addressing quota issues directly. I also plan to include a billing documentation link within the error message to offer more user support. I will also revisit `geminiService.ts`, ensuring API key freshness right before calls.


**Revising API Key Handling**

I'm now focusing on refining how API keys are managed and utilized, especially concerning the `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` error. I'll modify `App.tsx` to handle this error specifically, offering a clear user-facing message and an "Update Key" button for quota issues with user-provided keys. I'm also addressing the requirement to obtain the API key exclusively from `process.env.API_KEY` and ensuring a reset function for the key. I will handle the selected API Key selection process. I will consider cleaner error handling in `geminiService.ts` to improve the overall error management.


**Enhancing User Feedback**

I'm now implementing the UI updates. I'll include a "Manage API Key" button within the error state to facilitate project/key switching. The error message will also have a billing documentation link. I'll provide improved visual feedback with a "Quota Exceeded" card, featuring an indigo "Changer de Projet / Clé API" button and a billing documentation link, with a professional UI. For 429 errors, I will parse the message and display a prominent quota notice.


**Improving Retry Logic**

I'm now integrating retry logic into the `processBrandingVisuals` function within `App.tsx`. Currently, if a 429 error occurs during one of the API calls in this function, the entire process halts. I'm focusing on making sure that the failure of a single API request within `processBrandingVisuals` doesn't bring the whole process down. I'll make the calls more resilient. I will handle the scenario gracefully.

entries.description: Amélioration de la gestion des erreurs API, détection spécifique des limites de quota (429) et ajout d'un bouton pour changer de clé API/projet comme requis par les directives Veo/Pro.
